summary: Backend-only testing of Gemini-backed endpoints (POST /api/grade behavioral+technical, POST /api/analyze-jd) plus GET /api/ and 422 validation. All 6 pytest cases passed against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Real LLM calls (Gemini 2.5 Flash via emergentintegrations) returned schema-valid JSON in every run.

minor.issue: In _gemini_json, the except json.JSONDecodeError branch references `response[:500] if 'response' in dir() else 'N/A'` — 'response' is always in dir() inside the function scope (Python locals), so the guard never triggers; if exception occurs before assignment it will raise NameError instead of logging 'N/A'. Cosmetic/log-only, no user-visible effect.

`logger` is assigned AFTER `_gemini_json` is defined (line 213 vs use at lines 104/107). Works only because Python resolves the global at call time. Move logging.basicConfig + logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) above the endpoint definitions for clarity.

action_items: Optional: move logger setup above endpoint definitions (cosmetic)
Optional: fix the `'response' in dir()` guard in JSONDecodeError handler — assign response to None before try, then check `response is not None`
critical_code_review_comments: EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is correctly loaded from .env and never logged.
JSON extraction (_extract_json) is robust: handles ```json fences and first { ... } block.
Gemini errors are wrapped to HTTP 502 with detail message — meets 'graceful, not silent 500' requirement.
Pydantic validation produces 422 on missing required fields (verified).
No MongoDB _id leakage (status endpoints already project {_id: 0}).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Live LLM tests cost a Gemini call each (~25-30s total). Re-run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_llm_endpoints.py -v. If a future run is flaky due to model JSON drift, check raw responses — _extract_json already strips fences and finds the first {...} block.
